segments.summary: formalizes the exact fixed-window gap certificate, the necessary tail-mass lower bound, and monotone transport of visible-prefix agreement to larger lookahead windows

now packages same-core interval/exact transport of the first visible mismatch boundary, aligned lower/upper endpoint propagation for that boundary, the raw tail-mass lower-bound inequality, the shifted coarse `k^(n+L) < modulus` sufficient condition, and the exact fixed-window certificate itself between the stripped core at `(n, L)` and the actual denominator at `(n + s, L)`

now consumes both the generic and same-core certificate transport layers to reprove finite carry/remainder visible-word agreement on shifted windows and to transport remainder-to-carry functionality plus finite transition compatibility in the exact same-core regime

now cover finite normalization, traced runs, aligned finite state records, observed finite remainder/carry state-pair projections, pointwise local carry-balance and remainder-balance equations, exact finite-window functional criteria for the observed state-pair lists, finite transition-compatibility theorems under those criteria, explicit finite conflict lemmas refuting the criteria when the observed pair list disagrees, exact same-core transport of observed remainder-to-carry functionality on shifted windows, and finite aligned outputs, but no global factorization, minimization, or canonical morphism theorem
